title: Executive Order 11076—Establishing the President's Advisory Commission on Narcotic and Drug Abuse

flags.justification: Seven members appointed 'from persons outside the executive branch,' purely advisory in function.
Clear structure with specific deadlines: an interim report by April 1, 1963, and a final report by November 1, 1963.
Single coherent purpose: narcotic and drug abuse policy recommendations.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Sec. 6 sets an explicit, hard termination date: 'shall terminate not later than December 31, 1963' — a clean, unambiguous sunset.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed; this creates a new advisory function.
A purely advisory body with independent membership; no ongoing third-party compliance regime is created.
A narrow, task-specific, time-limited commission with no ongoing authority claimed beyond its report.
Sec. 3's liaison-designation directive to five named departments is coordination, not mandate redirection.

Sec. 5's exemption from civil service and classification laws for Commission staffing is a narrow, standard provision for temporary advisory-body personnel, consistent with similar bodies elsewhere in this corpus (e.g. EO 10938).

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/16 = 0.0%. Another exemplary time-limited independent advisory commission, joining EO 9387, EO 9808, and (partially) EO 10938 as clean designs of this type.
